# 🎯 Task 02.12: Snippet Generation & Query-Aware Summarization

## 📅 Sprint Info
- **Duration:** 4 days
- **Milestone:** M1 - Retrieval
- **Priority:** P0 (Critical Path — directly visible to users)
- **Depends On:** Task 02.1 (BM25), Task 01.7 (Persian Morphology), Task 01.3 (Script Processing)
- **Blocks:** Task 09.8 (End-to-End Pipeline)
- **Assignee:** TBD

## 🎬 What You'll Build
Implement query-aware snippet extraction for search results: select the most relevant passage from a document, highlight query terms (including morphological variants), truncate to 150-200 characters, and handle RTL (Persian/Arabic) and LTR (English) text. Snippets are what users read first — poor snippets directly harm CTR and perceived quality even for perfectly ranked results.

## 🧠 Why This Matters
A snippet is the user's first contact with a search result. Google invests heavily in snippet quality because:
- Users read the snippet to decide whether to click
- Poor snippets → low CTR → negative implicit feedback signal
- Snippets must highlight query terms so users immediately understand relevance
- For Persian text, correct RTL rendering and ZWNJ handling is required
- Snippets must be extracted from the correct content region (not nav/header/footer)

## 📋 Daily Breakdown

### Day 1: Content Region & Passage Extraction
- [ ] Extract main content region from stored HTML (strip nav, header, footer, ads)
- [ ] Segment document into passages (2-4 sentences per passage)
- [ ] Build passage relevance scorer (token overlap with query)
- [ ] Handle structured text (headings, lists, paragraphs)
- [ ] Store passages in MongoDB at index time for fast query-time retrieval
- [ ] Apply to 10K+ test documents, validate extraction quality manually (50 samples)
- [ ] Unit tests: 40+ extraction scenarios

### Day 2: Query-Aware Snippet Selection
- [ ] For each retrieved document, select best matching passage
- [ ] Score passages by: (a) query term coverage, (b) sentence position, (c) information density
- [ ] Add morphological variant matching (integrates Task 01.7)
- [ ] Handle empty / low-relevance passages: fall back to meta description
- [ ] Second fallback: first 200 chars of main content
- [ ] Handle missing/empty content gracefully
- [ ] Benchmark: <5ms per document for snippet extraction

### Day 3: Term Highlighting & RTL Rendering
- [ ] Implement query term highlighting (`<em>` / `<mark>` tags)
- [ ] Highlight morphological variants of query terms (not just exact match)
- [ ] Correct RTL wrapping for Persian/Arabic snippets
- [ ] Handle mixed RTL/LTR (Persian query, English snippet)
- [ ] ZWNJ-aware truncation (do not truncate inside ligature)
- [ ] Truncate at word boundary ≤200 chars, add `…`
- [ ] Render highlights safely (prevent XSS via HTML escaping before highlighting)
- [ ] Test with 20+ languages

### Day 4: Integration & Performance
- [ ] Integrate into query pipeline (after ranking, before response serialization)
- [ ] Add snippet quality metrics (coverage score, highlight rate)
- [ ] Performance: <5ms per document, <50ms total for 10 results
- [ ] Caching: cache extracted passages per document (Redis, TTL=1hr)
- [ ] End-to-end integration tests (50+ query scenarios)
- [ ] Visual regression tests for RTL rendering
- [ ] Documentation

## ✅ Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Relevant passage selected (manual precision ≥85% on 100-query evaluation)
- [ ] Query terms highlighted in snippet (≥90% coverage)
- [ ] Morphological variant highlighting works for Persian
- [ ] RTL direction correctly set for Persian/Arabic snippets
- [ ] Snippet length: 150-200 chars (no mid-word truncation)
- [ ] Meta description fallback when content unavailable
- [ ] XSS-safe: all HTML escaped before highlight tags inserted
- [ ] Latency: <5ms per document, <50ms for full result set
- [ ] Zero-result snippets handled gracefully

## 🧪 Testing Checklist
- [ ] Passage extraction tests (40+ HTML structures)
- [ ] Query term coverage tests (30+ query patterns)
- [ ] Morphological variant highlighting tests (20+ Persian cases)
- [ ] RTL rendering tests (Persian, Arabic, Hebrew)
- [ ] Truncation boundary tests (no mid-word, no mid-ZWNJ)
- [ ] XSS injection tests (malicious HTML in content)
- [ ] Performance benchmarks (<5ms per doc)
- [ ] Fallback scenario tests (empty content, no meta)

## 📊 Required Evidence
- Manual evaluation: 100 snippets rated for relevance and highlight quality (precision ≥85%).
- RTL rendering screenshot for 10+ Persian queries.
- XSS test results: show that `<script>` in document content is escaped.
- Latency benchmark: P50/P95 per document and per result page.
- Fallback coverage: count of documents using passage vs meta description vs first-200 fallback.

## 🎉 Celebration Criteria (Definition of Done)
✅ **Demo Ready:** Show search result page with highlighted snippets for a Persian query
✅ **Metric Met:** ≥85% manual relevance precision, <5ms per document
✅ **Integration:** Snippet appears in every search result in API response
✅ **Quality:** Persian RTL rendering correct; no XSS vulnerabilities

**🎊 Celebration Moment:** Search results now look and feel like a real search engine!

## 📦 Deliverables
- `include/snippet/SnippetExtractor.h` (C++ header, 80 lines)
- `src/snippet/SnippetExtractor.cpp` (C++ impl, 400 lines)
- `src/snippet/PassageScorer.cpp` (relevance scoring, 250 lines)
- `src/snippet/TermHighlighter.cpp` (highlighting + RTL, 200 lines)
- `tests/test_snippet_extractor.cpp` (50+ cases)
- `tests/test_term_highlighter.cpp` (40+ cases)
- `docs/snippet-generation-guide.md`

## 🔗 API

```cpp
struct Snippet {
    std::string text;           // Snippet text (≤200 chars)
    std::string highlighted;    // HTML with <em> highlighted terms
    bool is_meta_fallback;      // true if from meta description
    float relevance_score;      // Passage relevance score
    std::string direction;      // "rtl" or "ltr"
};

class SnippetExtractor {
public:
    Snippet extract(const Document& doc, const Query& query);
    std::vector<Snippet> extractBatch(
        const std::vector<Document>& docs, const Query& query);
};
```

## ⚠️ Security Note
Always HTML-escape document content BEFORE inserting `<em>` tags. Never trust raw HTML from crawled documents. Use allow-list approach for highlight tags only.
